title: leptophyllochloa micrathera
intro: Leptophyllochloa micrathera, also known as a species of grass, is a type of plant that belongs to the Poaceae family. Research on this plant is limited, but it has been reported to possess some medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could potentially be used to treat various health issues. However, as the information about Leptophyllochloa micrathera is scarce, there is limited evidence on its specific health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential uses and herbal preparations of this plant.
intro_study: According to "Photodermatology, photoimmunology & photomedicine", leptophyllochloa micrathera (presumably referring to Polypodium leucotomos as there is no reference to leptophyllochloa micrathera in the study) benefits include providing photoprotective effects, increasing the UV dose required for immediate pigment darkening, minimal erythema dose, and minimal phototoxic dose, and protecting Langherhans cells.
